title: Tutorial onboarding and first-run player path
summary: Build the M7 first-run onboarding path for external players, covering the existing pillars, platform surfaces and structured explanations without making UI authoritative.
acceptance: Provides an external-player first-run path covering launch, movement and input, time control, residents and work, hauling/building, saving, events, lamps, Chronicle, town rules, evidence and structured failure explanations
Adds or updates automated smoke coverage for the onboarding path on the relevant Web/Windows shell surfaces
Preserves Simulation Worker or Node headless as the only authoritative world writer; UI, Pixi, React and Electron remain read-model consumers
Does not add M8-scale tutorial content, public release flows, telemetry, accounts, paid services or public feedback systems
Records player-facing copy limits and unresolved onboarding risks in the task report

history.details: Created by WM-0098 M7 planning DAG; remains proposed until WM-0098 is verified, integrated and done.
Implemented read-model-only M7 first-run onboarding for Web/Windows shell HUD; added Web and desktop smoke coverage; recorded copy limits/risks; required checks pass.
verified: No blocking findings; acceptance evidence confirmed for read-model-only Web/Windows first-run onboarding, smoke coverage, roadmap guardrails and M7 non-goals.
Verified WM-0099 integrated after handoff validation, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, pnpm typecheck, Web/desktop shell e2e and pnpm quality passed.

objective: Make the first external-player session understandable without changing simulation authority or M6 product-gate verdicts.
forbiddenPaths: telemetry accounts paid services crash upload or public feedback system
public release upload store submission signing installer or updater
M8 task creation, promotion, claim, implementation or review
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: onboarding copy can overclaim Web or Windows readiness; preserve the M6 verdict language.
Risk: UI tutorial work can drift into authority changes; all commands must still flow through approved command surfaces.
File ownership is limited to onboarding UI/shell surfaces, player-facing design docs and WM-0099 reports.
ownerGate: Block on any public release, store, signing, telemetry, account, paid service, crash upload or public feedback requirement
Block if onboarding requires UI or Electron to mutate authoritative world state directly
Block if the task needs new M8 content volume or changes M6 Web/Windows verdicts
m0M6RegressionCoverage: Preserve M6 Web demo-only and Windows controlled-external-test verdicts
Run shell smoke coverage without weakening existing M0-M6 gates
benchmarkImpact: No benchmark baseline update expected. Any measurable shell performance regression over existing thresholds must be explained or fixed.
reviewRouting: client-engineer completes to reviewer; reviewer verifies the first-run path, automated smoke evidence, authority boundaries and M7 non-goals.
rollbackModel: Revert onboarding UI/shell/doc changes and WM-0099 reports; leave the M7 DAG and other proposed tasks intact.

sparkEligibility.reason: Cross-surface onboarding touches product UX, platform verdict language and external-player readiness.

review.summary: No blocking findings; acceptance evidence confirmed for read-model-only Web/Windows first-run onboarding, smoke coverage, roadmap guardrails and M7 non-goals.

integration.summary: Verified WM-0099 integrated after handoff validation, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, pnpm typecheck, Web/desktop shell e2e and pnpm quality passed.
